text: Bank earnings live updates: JPMorgan, BofA, Goldman, Citi and Wells Fargo are set to report - CNBC
fullText: The five banks releasing earnings Tuesday morning are expected to report strong revenue from trading equities and fixed income.

text: OpenAI is breaking Silicon Valley's unwritten code. That's why Apple is so angry. - Business Insider
fullText: OpenAI is defying Silicon Valley's oldest playbook, helping explain why Apple is fighting so hard.

text: Tyler Perry Claims Male Sexual Assault Accuser Proposed Multi-Million Dollar Settlement - TMZ
fullText: Tyler Perry claims his male sexual assault accuser, Mario Rodriguez, is working overtime to pressure him into a settlement … but the famed director says he won't cave.

text: Deputy U.S. marshal shot dead while serving arrest warrant on fugitive in Louisiana, agency says - CBS News
fullText: A deputy U.S. marshal was shot and killed while serving an arrest warrant on a fugitive in Louisiana, authorities say. The suspect is in custody.

text: Horoscope for Tuesday, July 14, 2026 - Chicago Sun-Times
The star-studded World Cup semifinals kick off with France vs. Spain - NPR
fullText: The expanded World Cup allowed FIFA to introduce a new seeding system that placed the world's top teams in opposite corners of the knockout bracket. Now, the semifinals are both heavyweight matchups.

text: China Expels Former Politburo Member Over Graft and Sex Charges - Bloomberg.com
fullText: China expelled former Politburo member Ma Xingrui from the ruling Communist Party, accusing him of a wide range of offenses including corruption, abuse of power and trading political favors for sex.

text: Mexico’s Sheinbaum announces criminal charges request in US over ICE deaths - Al Jazeera
fullText: Mexico to formally ask prosecutors to file cases in US courts over its citizens’ deaths in immigration operations.

text: Oil prices hit 1-month high as US-Iran attacks dim Strait of Hormuz outlook - Al Jazeera
fullText: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran.

text: Erling Haaland's substitute World Cup trophy: A taxidermy raccoon - USA Today
fullText: Norwegian soccer superstar Erling Haaland was seen with a curious critter when his team landed in Oslo on July 13. Turns out it was a taxidermy raccoon holding a bottle of booze.

text: DHS: ICE officer shot man in Maine after ‘fearing for public safety’ - The Hill
fullText: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said Monday the federal immigration officer who fatally shot a man in Maine did so out of fear for “public safety.” The department wrote on X that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers were conducting “ta…

text: People killed in Bangkok music bar fire were found trapped in windowless bathrooms - AP News
fullText: Most of the people who were killed in a huge fire in a Bangkok music bar were found trapped in windowless bathrooms where they may have sought to escape the flames that claimed at least 27 lives. That's what Thai authorities said Monday as investigations bega…

text: Trump's Hormuz toll plans bring oil supply risks back in spotlight - CNBC
fullText: While the proposed levy would raise shipping costs, an escalating conflict could remove barrels from the market altogether.

text: Hours each day in an iron lung kept her breathing, her will to live kept her alive - BBC
fullText: The sister of the last known polio patient in the US to use the life-saving machine spoke to the BBC about her sister's life.

text: Parasitic outbreak of cyclosporiasis linked in Michigan to lettuce - The Detroit News
fullText: Michigan health officials said they have not yet pinpointed the grower or supplier where the outbreak originated.

text: Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to serve the rest of his Senate term - The Washington Post
fullText: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) announced his selection of Darline Graham Nordone to serve into January. She is expected to be sworn in Tuesday.

text: Scoop: Trump gave Saudi crown prince his backing for risky strikes on Houthis - Axios
fullText: President Trump signed off on a highly unusual Saudi strike against Iran-backed Houthis.

text: Oil Holds Gains as Iran Stokes Inflation Worries: Markets Wrap - Bloomberg.com
fullText: US stocks steadied as traders refrained from making big bets ahead of a jam-packed day that will see the unofficial start of the earnings season, along with testimony from Fed Chair Kevin Warsh and US inflation data.
